IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified poles that need replacing and upgrading. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our contractors Avidety may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. To allow our contractors to safely replace 4 poles in your area, we will need to temporarily disconnect the generator on 17th July 2026. Once the work on 17th July 2026 is finished, the temporary generator will be reconnected. After all planned works have been completed, we will then disconnect the generator on 21st July 2026 and return your supply to the main power network. This work is taking place in and around the Wingmore area.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area.Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely cut trees away from the overhead cables. By carefully planning this work in advance, we have been able to minimise the duration of the power interruption. This essential work will cover approximately 100 metres of tree cutting across 5 sections of the overhead network.
Doing this helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months.This work is taking place near Wingham Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: A third party has arranged for trees near the overhead electricity lines to be trimmed and has asked us to temporarily switch off the power so this can be carried out safely. Some of these trees have grown close to the cables and could cause damage, particularly during poor weather. Allowing this work to take place helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts as we appreciate you may have been impacted by an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so a tree surgeon can safely cut trees away from the overhead cables. This work will help reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts in the future. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations.

To talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Decasta at 04:15 - Thanks for your patience this morning.

Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our overhead cabling network. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely cut trees away from the overhead cables. By carefully planning this work in advance, we have been able to minimise the duration of the power interruption. This essential work will cover approximately 30 metres of tree cutting across 1 section of the overhead network.
Doing this helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months.This work is taking place off Wouldham Road.

Our staff are trying to find the root cause and fix it permanently, however with this kind of issue it's likely that power will go off multiple times before we're able to find and fix it. For now we have restored your power as quickly as we can.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Crown Street area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our estimated timeframe to restore power is between 01:30-02:30. I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Hi, it's David from the messaging team at UK Power Networks. I understand how difficult it can be without power and the additional worries this can bring.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Decasta at 03:16 - Thanks for bearing with us tonight.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105.
